questions.prompt: Under no circumstances ___ reveal this information.
Had I known about the traffic, I ___ left earlier.
questions.explanation: Third conditional inversion: 'Had I...' instead of 'If I had...'

questions.prompt: The proposal was rejected ___ the basis of the cost.
questions.explanation: 'On the basis of' is the correct preposition phrase.

questions.prompt: But for your help, we ___ never succeeded.
questions.explanation: 'But for' + noun = 'If it hadn't been for', triggers conditional.

questions.prompt: Little ___ know that this decision would change everything.
Contrary to expectations, the experiment ___.
questions.explanation: Past simple for completed action that surprised.

questions.prompt: Not until much later ___ the truth.
There is no point ___ about the past.
___ the heavy rain, the match continued.
The findings are consistent ___ previous research.
Only after careful consideration ___ our decision.
She resented ___ for the mistake.
Were I to win the lottery, I ___ around the world.
The committee is composed ___ experts from various fields.
In no way ___ responsible for this outcome.
We have no option but ___.
Such was his confidence that he ___ fail.
The theory fails to account ___ these anomalies.
Nowhere in the document ___ mentioned.
We are committed ___ the highest standards.
She ___ have studied harder for the exam.
questions.explanation: 'Should have' expresses past criticism or regret about something that didn't happen.

questions.prompt: It was John who ___ the mistake, not anyone else.
questions.explanation: Cleft sentence: 'It was [subject] who [verb]' for emphasis.

questions.prompt: The man ___ car was stolen called the police.
He said he ___ visit us the following week.
questions.explanation: Future in the past: 'would' for future actions from a past perspective.

questions.prompt: The robbery is thought ___ by an insider.
questions.explanation: Passive with reporting verb: 'is thought + perfect infinitive' for past action.

questions.prompt: ___ worked harder, I would have passed the exam.
questions.explanation: Inverted conditional: 'Had I' instead of 'If I had'.

questions.prompt: Not only ___ late, but he also forgot the documents.
questions.explanation: 'Not only' triggers inversion with auxiliary before subject.

questions.prompt: Having finished the report, she ___ home.
questions.explanation: Participle clause showing completed action before main clause.

questions.prompt: You ___ have bought tickets; they were free.
questions.explanation: 'Needn't have' means you did something unnecessary.

questions.prompt: What I need is a long holiday, not more work.
questions.explanation: Cleft sentence: 'What I need is X' for emphasis.

questions.prompt: They denied ___ the confidential data.
questions.explanation: 'Deny + perfect gerund' for action before the time of denying.

questions.prompt: The house, ___ was built in 1920, needs renovation.
Rarely ___ such a beautiful sunset.
I remember ___ to the park as a child.
questions.explanation: Passive gerund after 'remember' for past memory.

questions.prompt: If you ___ me, I would have helped you.
questions.explanation: Third conditional: past perfect in if-clause.

questions.prompt: We objected ___ treated so rudely.
Seldom ___ such dedication from employees.
___ accept their offer, I would have regretted it.
questions.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had I' instead of 'If I had'.

questions.prompt: The project is believed ___ over budget.
questions.explanation: Passive reporting verb with perfect infinitive for past result.

questions.prompt: It was in Paris that they first ___.
questions.explanation: Cleft sentence emphasizing place with 'It was...that...'
